audit_status.source_base: Greek 2 Peter text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for false teachers, exploitation, Noah/Lot examples, arrogance, corruption, return imagery. Strong polemic held as warning against predatory teaching.

verses.restored: But false prophets also came among the people, as also false teachers will be among you, who will secretly bring in destructive sects/opinions, even denying the Master who bought them, bringing swift destruction upon themselves.
And many will follow their licentious ways, because of whom the way of truth will be blasphemed.
And in greed they will exploit you with fabricated words; their judgment from long ago is not idle, and their destruction does not sleep.
For if the Divine did not spare messengers who sinned/misaligned, but cast them into gloomy pits, handing them over to be kept for judgment,
and did not spare ancient world but guarded Noah, eighth, herald of righteousness/justice, bringing flood upon world of irreverent ones,
and condemned cities of Sodom and Gomorrah to ruin, reducing them to ashes, setting example of what is coming to irreverent ones,
and rescued righteous/just Lot, worn down by conduct of lawless ones in licentiousness,
for by seeing and hearing, the righteous/just one dwelling among them day by day was tormenting righteous/just soul/life with their lawless works,
then Lord knows to rescue reverent ones from testing and to keep unjust ones for day of judgment being corrected,
especially those going after flesh in defiling desire and despising lordship. Bold, self-willed, they do not tremble blaspheming glories,
whereas messengers, being greater in strength and power, do not bring against them blasphemous judgment before Lord.
But these, like irrational living creatures born by nature for capture and decay, blaspheming in things they do not know, will also decay in their corruption,
being wronged as wages of wrongdoing; considering daytime indulgence pleasure, stains and blemishes, reveling in their deceits while feasting with you,
having eyes full of adultery and unceasing from sin/misalignment, enticing unstable souls/lives, having a heart trained in greed, children of curse.
Leaving straight way, they wandered, following way of Balaam son of Bosor, who loved wages of wrongdoing,
but had rebuke for his own lawlessness: a voiceless donkey, speaking in human voice, restrained the prophet's madness.
These are waterless springs and mists driven by storm, for whom gloom of darkness has been kept.
For speaking swollen empty things, they entice in fleshly desires and licentiousness those barely escaping from those living in error.
Promising them freedom while they themselves are enslaved to corruption; for by what someone is overcome, to this one is enslaved.

For if, having escaped defilements of the world by knowledge of Lord and Savior Jesus Messiah, and being entangled again in these, they are overcome, the last things have become worse for them than the first.
For it would have been better for them not to have known way of righteousness/justice than, having known, to turn back from holy command handed over to them.
It has happened to them according to true proverb: Dog returned to its own vomit, and washed sow to wallowing in mud.

safety_note: 2 Peter 2 is severe polemic against exploitative false teachers. Do not use animal imagery or judgment language to dehumanize vulnerable people or ordinary opponents. The target is predatory, greedy, deceptive leadership that exploits unstable souls/lives.
translation_notes: Sodom/Gomorrah is framed around lawlessness and predatory injustice in this passage; avoid later sexualized prooftext misuse.
False freedom is exposed as enslavement to corruption.
